source_paper_title: Joint Mixability and Notions of Negative Dependence
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Koike_2024, title={Joint Mixability and Notions of Negative Dependence}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0121}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0121}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Koike, Takaaki and Lin, Liyuan and Wang, Ruodu}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2786–2802} }
context:
Section 6 (Conclusion), page 19 (in the provided parsed text; labeled page 19/32).

Assuming homogeneous marginals, does an exchangeable joint mix solve problem (8) for a general convex cost function f? In Theorem 4, we showed that this holds true for quadratic cost. We also know that a joint mix is an optimizer for the general convex cost problem without uncertainty. These observations seem to hint at the possible optimality of some exchangeable joint mix for general convex cost under uncertainty, but we do not have a proof.
title: Extend exchangeable joint-mix optimality for uncertain subsets under general convex costs
background_latex:
Let \(n\ge 2\) and let \([n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\). Let \(F\) be a univariate probability distribution on \(\mathbb{R}\) with finite second moment, and consider random vectors \(X=(X_1,\dots,X_n)\) such that \(X_i\sim F\) for all \(i\in[n]\) (identical marginals).

For a subset \(K\subseteq[n]\), define the partial sum \(S_K(X)=\sum_{i\in K} X_i\), with the convention \(S_\varnothing(X)=0\). Let \(f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) be a convex measurable function such that the relevant expectations are finite.

Let \(2^{[n]}\) denote the power set of \([n]\). For a probability measure \(\mu\) on \(2^{[n]}\), define the uncertainty-averaged cost functional
\[
C_f^\mu(X):=\sum_{K\subseteq[n]} \mathbb{E}\bigl[f(S_K(X))\bigr]\,\mu(K).
\]
Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a nonempty set of probability measures on \(2^{[n]}\) (an "uncertainty set"). Call \(\mathcal{M}\) symmetric if for every \(\mu\in\mathcal{M}\) and every permutation \(\pi\) of \([n]\), the permuted measure \(\mu^\pi\) defined by \(\mu^\pi(K)=\mu(\pi(K))\) also lies in \(\mathcal{M}\), where \(\pi(K)=\{\pi(i): i\in K\}\).

The robust multi-marginal transport problem under subset uncertainty is
\[
\inf\Bigl\{\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal{M}} C_f^\mu(X):\ X_i\sim F\ \text{for all } i\in[n]\Bigr\}.
\]
A random vector \(X\) is exchangeable if \((X_1,\dots,X_n)\) is distributionally invariant under permutations of coordinates. A joint mix is a vector with \(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i=c\) almost surely for some constant \(c\) (for identical marginals this is tied to complete mixability of \(F\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Assume \(F\) is \(n\)-completely mixable (so that at least one joint mix with marginals \(F\) exists) and assume \(\mathcal{M}\) is symmetric.

Determine whether, for every convex measurable \(f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) (with finite expectations), there exists an exchangeable joint mix \(X\) with marginals \(F\) that attains the optimum of the robust problem
\[
\inf\Bigl\{\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal{M}} \sum_{K\subseteq[n]} \mathbb{E}\bigl[f(S_K(X))\bigr]\,\mu(K):\ X_i\sim F\ \text{for all } i\in[n]\Bigr\}.
\]
Equivalently: characterize conditions under which some exchangeable joint mix solves the above robust optimization for general convex \(f\).

Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a nonempty set of probability measures on \(2^{[n]}\) (an "uncertainty set"). Call \(\mathcal{M}\) symmetric if for every \(\mu\in\mathcal{M}\) and every permutation \(\pi\) of \([n]\), the permuted measure \(\mu^\pi\) defined by \(\mu^\pi(K)=\mu(\pi(K))\) also lies in \(\mathcal{M}\), where \(\pi(K)=\{\pi(i): i\in K\}\).

Assume \(F\) is \(n\)-completely mixable (so that at least one joint mix with marginals \(F\) exists) and assume \(\mathcal{M}\) is symmetric.

Determine whether, for every convex measurable \(f:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) (with finite expectations), there exists an exchangeable joint mix \(X\) with marginals \(F\) that attains the optimum of the robust problem
\[

location_in_paper: Section 6 (Conclusion), page 19 (in the provided parsed text; labeled page 19/32).
open_problem_quote: Assuming homogeneous marginals, does an exchangeable joint mix solve problem (8) for a general convex cost function f? In Theorem 4, we showed that this holds true for quadratic cost. We also know that a joint mix is an optimizer for the general convex cost problem without uncertainty. These observations seem to hint at the possible optimality of some exchangeable joint mix for general convex cost under uncertainty, but we do not have a proof.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: For fixed small \(n\), one can discretize \(F\), choose candidate convex functions \(f\), and compute/approximate \(\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal{M}} C_f^\mu(X)\) over parametrized couplings (e.g., via linear programming on finite supports), making automated searches for counterexamples plausible. However, the space of convex \(f\) and uncertainty sets \(\mathcal{M}\) is large, and proving nonexistence would still require theoretical arguments beyond a finite search.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate couplings (including exchangeable ones) can be represented algorithmically (e.g., via transport plans on grids or copula parametrizations), and a fitness signal can be the computed robust objective \(\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal{M}} C_f^\mu(X)\) for chosen \(f\) and \(\mathcal{M}\). Evolutionary search could discover couplings that outperform exchangeable joint mixes on discretized instances or suggest extremal structures, but scaling and the need to cover general convex \(f\) limit full reliability.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The robust subset-uncertainty transport problem is introduced and analyzed in Koike–Lin–Wang (2024) in the homogeneous, symmetric-uncertainty setting. When \(F\) is \(n\)-completely mixable, joint mixes exist and are optimal for the classical (non-robust) Monge–Kantorovich objective \(\mathbb E[f(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i)]\) for convex \(f\) (Wang–Wang 2016). Under subset uncertainty, Koike–Lin–Wang show that for quadratic cost \(f(x)=x^2\) the robust objective is minimized by exchangeable negatively correlated joint mixes with correlation matrix having off-diagonal entries \(-1/(n-1)\) (Theorems 4–5), leveraging symmetry of \(\mathcal M\) and exchangeabilization arguments.

For general convex \(f\), however, the paper explicitly leaves open whether some exchangeable joint mix remains robustly optimal under symmetric uncertainty sets \(\mathcal M\). Existing foundational works provide ingredients but not a resolution: joint mixability theory explains why constant-sum couplings are optimal for convex functions of the full sum, while multi-marginal OT theory supplies tools for symmetric costs, but the robust \(\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal M}\) over subset-indexed costs introduces a max-type nonlinearity that breaks the direct Jensen argument. At present there is no forward-citation evidence of a solution, so the problem remains open; progress likely requires either identifying classes of convex \(f\) and symmetric \(\mathcal M\) for which exchangeable joint mixes are minimax optimal, or constructing counterexamples where non-joint-mix couplings outperform all exchangeable joint mixes under worst-case subset selection.

sources.title: Joint mixability and notions of negative dependence
sources.theorem_or_result: Formulates the robust subset-uncertainty multi-marginal transport problem \(\inf_X \sup_{\mu\in\mathcal M}\sum_{K\subseteq[n]}\mathbb E[f(S_K(X))]\mu(K)\) and proves optimality of exchangeable NCD joint mixes for quadratic cost \(f(x)=x^2\) when \(F\) is \(n\)-completely mixable and \(\mathcal M\) is symmetric (Theorems 4–5). It explicitly leaves open whether an exchangeable joint mix is optimal for general convex \(f\) under symmetric uncertainty (Open Question 4).
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing exactly the open problem: extend the quadratic-cost optimality of exchangeable joint mixes under symmetric subset uncertainty to general convex costs, or characterize when such optimality holds.

sources.title: Maxmin expected utility with non-unique prior
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the maxmin expected utility criterion \(\max_{a}\min_{p\in\mathcal P} \mathbb E_p[u(a)]\), providing the decision-theoretic foundation for robust optimization via \(\sup_{\mu\in\mathcal M}\) over a set of priors.
sources.connection: Provides the conceptual justification for taking a worst case over subset-selection measures \(\mu\in\mathcal M\) in the robust transport objective, but does not address joint mixability or convex-cost optimality.

sources.title: Joint mixability
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops the theory of joint mixability and shows that when a joint mix exists, it minimizes \(\mathbb E[f(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i)]\) for convex \(f\) (and is essentially characterized by strict convexity). Provides necessary conditions such as \(2\max_i \sigma_i \le \sum_i \sigma_i\) for existence of a joint mix.
sources.connection: Supplies the baseline (non-robust, no subset uncertainty) convex-cost optimality of joint mixes, which motivates asking whether joint-mix optimality persists under subset uncertainty and worst-case \(\mu\).

sources.title: Extremal dependence concepts
sources.theorem_or_result: Surveys extremal positive/negative dependence, including complete/joint mixability and countermonotonicity, and connects them to optimization of convex functionals of sums and risk aggregation bounds.
sources.connection: Provides broader context and tools around joint mixability as extremal negative dependence and its role in convex optimization, but does not treat the robust subset-uncertainty objective.

sources.title: Centers of probability measures without the mean
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows that for \(n\)-completely mixable \(F\), there exists an exchangeable joint mix with marginals \(F\) (and studies centers beyond the mean).
sources.connection: Ensures feasibility of restricting attention to exchangeable joint mixes when \(F\) is \(n\)-completely mixable, a key structural ingredient in the open problem.

sources.title: Multi-marginal optimal transport: Theory and applications
sources.theorem_or_result: Surveys multi-marginal optimal transport, including structure/uniqueness for costs depending on sums and repulsive costs, and links to convexity and symmetry considerations.
sources.connection: Gives OT background relevant to interpreting the robust problem as a multi-marginal transport with a family of costs indexed by subsets, but does not resolve the convex-cost robust optimality question.
